CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Continued To Aug-19-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

COMPLIANCE HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-04-2026 Continued To Jul-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Gabriel Pimentel, Esq. 626-765-6505 Pimentel Law Group for Plaintiff.
Daisy Chen, Esq. CDF Labor Law 916-361-0991 for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
The Court admonishes Plaintiff's counsel; draws counsel's attention to OSC order filed 12/18/25 on lines 27 & 28; violation of April and December orders, courtesy copies missing,
Mr. Pimental responds to the court's query; states timeline and sequence of events.
Court comments; takes PAGA seriously; fiduciary duty to absent folks; inattenton to details; complete disclosure; needs information and complete accounting.

After full arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling on both matters.

1 - COMPLIANCE HEARING.

On calendar is a Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement) and Order to Show Cause. The compliance hearing is continued to July 8, 2026, at 9:00 am in this department. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to pay sanctions of $500.

(see word minutes for full details)

Prior to the time set for this hearing, Plaintiff is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text. Plaintiff shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

OSC HEARING (ADDED PER ORDER FILED 12/18/25)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Continued To Apr-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Continued To Mar-04-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

COMPLIANCE HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Continued To Mar-04-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Jenna Koleson, Esq. 626-765-6505 Pimental Law for Plaintiff.
Joel Van Parys, Esq. CDF Labor Law LLP for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

Hearing is held. Court and Counsel discuss declarations filed. Ms. Koleson apologizes, misunderstanding, error. The Court looks at the ROA and does not find a supplemental declaration filed by Plaintiff after 4/1/25. Mr. Vanparys responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

COMPLIANCE HEARING.

On calendar is a Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement).

The court orders Plaintiff's counsel to show cause why they should not be held in contempt or ordered to pay $500 in sanctions to the clerk of the court or both for violating the court's April 29, 2025 Order. Specifically, on April 29, 2025, the court ordered "Plaintiff's counsel to file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing no later than December 8, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing." (4/29/2025 Order at p. 1.) Plaintiff's counsel did neither. The OSC will be heard on March 4, 2026, at 9 am in this department. Plaintiff's counsel of record's personal appearance is required at the OSC hearing. Any response to the OSC must be filed no later than February 18, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing.

The Settlement Compliance Hearing (PAGA Settlement) is continued to March 4, 2026, at 9 am in this department. Plaintiff's counsel must file a declaration re disposition of settlement proceeds and any other outstanding issues that may be discussed at the hearing no later than February 18, 2025, with courtesy copy provided to the department within 24 hours of filing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
